html:
Logs are basic building materials with multiple purposes. They can be Refined into Boards, which opens up more recipes.

Logs are dropped from Evergreens, Mushtrees, Cave Banana Trees and Birchnut Trees after cutting the trees with an Axe. The larger the Tree, the more logs are dropped. There are three evergreen tree sizes, which yield 1, 2 or 3 logs depending on the size.

Tree Stumps from cut Evergreens and Mushtrees can be dug using a Shovel to get one extra log. Spiky Trees can also be cut with a chance of getting a log and another one when dug up. Mining full Tall Stalagmites may also yield a log.

In Werebeaver form, Woodie can eat Logs to replenish his Log meter.

Contents

  • 1 Downloadable Content
  • 2 Usage
  • 3 Tips
  • 4 Bugs
  • 5 Gallery

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants DLC, Birchnut Trees drop Logs.

In the Shipwrecked DLC, Logs can be obtained by chopping down Palm Trees, Jungle Trees, as well as by rolling three carrots at the Slot Machine. Logs take on a more saturated coloration in this DLC.

In the Hamlet DLC, Logs can be obtained from Rainforest Trees, or Tea Trees, as well as by hammering A Smashing Pot. Logs take on a brighter color in this DLC.

×4

Tips

  • A very effective method of harvesting Logs in the Reign of Giants DLC is to summon Bigfoot and let it stomp on a group of tightly planted Evergreens or Birchnut Trees.

Bugs

  • There are actually two types of logs in the Hamlet DLC ("Rainforest Log" and "Teatree Log"). For some reason, the log icon in the inventory is "Teatree Log" instead of "Rainforest Log" since March 18, 2019 update; this also explains why the logs in the inventory are so different from those on the ground. The original "Rainforest Log" icon has been removed for no reason.

Gallery

  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Unused artwork for a Diseased Log in Don't Starve Together.

  • Unused Teatree Log on the ground in the Hamlet DLC

“It's big, it's heavy, and it's wood.”
–Wilson
“Wood! So flammable! Hooray!”
–Willow
“Is thick like my arm.”
–Wolfgang
“If trees could talk, they would scream at the sight of this.”
–Wendy
“LOG(1) = 0”

“An axial section of tree trunk.”
–Wickerbottom
“It makes it all worthwhile.”
–Woodie
“It's a piece of wood. What more is there to say?”
–Maxwell
“Cross-section of a tree. For fuel and construction purposes.”
–Wagstaff
“Wööd is always öf value.”

“Could use some more of these.”

“It's a hunk of wood.”
–Winona
“A piece of what was once a tree.”
–Wortox
“Can we make campfire?”
–Wurt
“Firewood!”
–Walter
“It came from a tree, as you'd expect.”
–Wanda
Logs are basic building materials with multiple purposes. They can be Refined into Boards, which opens up more recipes.
Logs are dropped from Evergreens, Mushtrees, Cave Banana Trees and Birchnut Trees after cutting the trees with an Axe. The larger the Tree, the more logs are dropped. There are three evergreen tree sizes, which yield 1, 2 or 3 logs depending on the size.
Tree Stumps from cut Evergreens and Mushtrees can be dug using a Shovel to get one extra log. Spiky Trees can also be cut with a chance of getting a log and another one when dug up. Mining full Tall Stalagmites may also yield a log.
In Werebeaver form, Woodie can eat Logs to replenish his Log meter.

Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Reign of Giants DLC, Birchnut Trees drop Logs.
In the Shipwrecked DLC, Logs can be obtained by chopping down Palm Trees, Jungle Trees, as well as by rolling three carrots at the Slot Machine. Logs take on a more saturated coloration in this DLC.
In the Hamlet DLC, Logs can be obtained from Rainforest Trees, or Tea Trees, as well as by hammering A Smashing Pot. Logs take on a brighter color in this DLC.

Tips[edit | edit source]
A very effective method of harvesting Logs in the Reign of Giants DLC is to summon Bigfoot and let it stomp on a group of tightly planted Evergreens or Birchnut Trees.
Bugs[edit | edit source]
There are actually two types of logs in the Hamlet DLC ("Rainforest Log" and "Teatree Log"). For some reason, the log icon in the inventory is "Teatree Log" instead of "Rainforest Log" since March 18, 2019 update; this also explains why the logs in the inventory are so different from those on the ground. The original "Rainforest Log" icon has been removed for no reason.

They can be [[Refined]] into [[Boards]], which opens up more recipes. Logs are dropped from [[Evergreens]], [[Mushtree]]s, [[Cave Banana Tree]]s and [[Birchnut Tree|Birchnut Trees]] after cutting the trees with an [[Axe]]. The larger the [[Tree]], the more logs are dropped. There are three evergreen tree sizes, which yield 1, 2 or 3 logs depending on the size. Mining full [[Stalagmite|Tall Stalagmites]] may also yield a log. In Werebeaver form, [[Woodie]] can eat Logs to replenish his Log meter. =={{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content== In the ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, Birchnut Trees drop Logs. Logs take on a more saturated coloration in this DLC. In the ''[[Hamlet]] ''DLC, Logs can be obtained from [[Rainforest Tree]]s, or [[Tea Tree]]s, as well as by hammering [[A Smashing Pot]].
